book_summary: Leviticus has chapters 1-23 complete in restored/source-audited draft and mystical companion layers. The book now covers offerings and reparation, priestly ordination and rupture, clean/unclean ritual states, body and illness safeguards, Day of Atonement, holiness and social justice, difficult sexual/penalty texts with anti-weaponization metadata, priestly holy-food and bodily-wholeness boundaries, and the appointed-times calendar of Sabbath, liberation memory, harvest, cleansing, joy, and justice for poor and sojourner.

chapters.notes: Appointed times: Sabbath, Passover/Unleavened Bread, firstfruits, Weeks, trumpet memorial, Day of Atonement, Booths, harvest justice.

translation_notes: Moedim/appoined times are sacred communal rhythms, not private status markers.
Weekly Sabbath frames the annual calendar with rest.
Passover and Unleavened Bread preserve Exodus liberation memory.
Firstfruits and Weeks sanctify harvest and gratitude before consumption.
Leviticus 23:22 inserts harvest justice directly into festival instruction: field edges and gleanings belong to poor and sojourner.
Day of Atonement soul-humbling must not become self-hatred.
Festival of Booths joins harvest joy to embodied wilderness memory.
Mystical companion layer remains interpretive and does not replace source-audited restored text.
source_witness_audit_flags: Leviticus 23 festival calendar requires comparison with Exodus, Numbers, and Deuteronomy festival traditions.
Leviticus 23 Sabbath and annual moedim require calendrical/source-layer audit.
Leviticus 23 firstfruits and Weeks counting require Hebrew calendrical and Second Temple reception audit.
Leviticus 23 trumpet memorial terminology requires lexical and liturgical-history audit.
Leviticus 23 Day of Atonement should crosslink with Leviticus 16.
Leviticus 23:22 harvest justice should crosslink with Leviticus 19:9-10 and Ruth.
Leviticus 23 Booths requires Exodus-memory and wilderness-dwelling audit.
next_actions: Continue Leviticus with chapter 24: continual lamp, bread of presence, blasphemy case, equal law for native and sojourner, and proportional justice material with anti-violence safeguards.
